title: Sketch-effect hatching
abstract: Computer efficiency improved sketch-effect hatching is provided. A hatching tool generates a bounding area associated with an object, within which a plurality of hatching line segment strokes is generated. The strokes are generated by generating center points on equally or near-equally spaced parallel columns within the bounding area that are vertically-offset from each other by randomly-generated stroke lengths. The center points are then randomly horizontally-offset from the columns according to a calculated deviation. A random angle deviation is derived, and end points of the strokes are calculated based on the new center points, stroke lengths, and angle deviations. The strokes are then enhanced by generating overstrokes. Further, the hatching tool maps the strokes to the object, rotates the strokes by a hatching angle, keystone-corrects the strokes, and clips the strokes to fit within the object's boundary. The strokes are output to provide a hand-sketched hatching effect.
claims:
Claims 1. A computer-implemented method to improve computer-efficiency for generating and applying sketch-effect hatching to an object, comprising: receiving an indication of a selection to apply the sketch-effect hatching to the object; determining a bounding area associated with the object for generating hatching strokes; within the bounding area, generating a plurality of hatching strokes along a plurality of equally or near-equally spaced parallel columns; perturbing each of the plurality of hatching strokes according to a randomly- generated angle deviation; and providing the object and the plurality of hatching strokes for output to a visual display or a printing device. 2. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein generating the plurality of hatching strokes along the plurality of equally or near-equally spaced parallel columns comprises generating the plurality of hatching strokes, wherein each of the plurality of hatching strokes has a randomly-generated stroke length. 3. The computer-implemented method of claim 2, further comprising: determining a stroke center for each of the plurality of hatching strokes; and offsetting the stroke centers for each of the plurality of hatching strokes along the parallel columns based on randomly-generated stroke lengths. 4. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising perturbing each of the plurality of hatching strokes along at least one axis according to a randomly- generated deviation. 5. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising: calculating endpoints for each of the plurality of hatching strokes; and mapping the plurality of hatching strokes to the object. 6. The computer-implemented method of claim 5, wherein mapping the plurality of hatching strokes to the object comprises: translating the plurality of hatching strokes to the object; uniformly rotating the plurality of hatching strokes by an angle according to a predetermined or user-selected fill angle; and keystone-correcting the plurality of hatching strokes according to a predetermined or user-selected gradient. 7. The computer-implemented method of claim 6, wherein receiving the indication of the selection to apply the sketch-effect hatching to the object comprises receiving sketch- effect hatching properties, the sketch-effect hatching properties defining the fill angle and the gradient. 8. A system for improving computer-efficiency for generating and applying sketch- effect hatching to an object, the system comprising a computing device, the computing device comprising: at least one processing device; and at least one computer readable data storage device storing instructions that, when executed by the at least one processing device are operative to provide a hatching tool operative to: receive an indication of a selection to apply the sketch-effect hatching to the object; determine a bounding area associated with the object for generating hatching strokes; within the bounding area, generate a plurality of hatching strokes along a plurality of equally or near-equally spaced parallel columns; perturb each of the plurality of hatching strokes according to a randomly- generated angle deviation; and provide an output of hatching stroke endpoints to a visual display for rendering the plurality of hatching strokes on the visual display or to a printing device for printing the object and the hatching strokes on a print medium. 9. The system of claim 8, wherein in generating the plurality of hatching strokes along the plurality of equally or near-equally spaced parallel columns, the hatching tool is operative to: determine a randomly -generated stroke length for each of the plurality of hatching strokes; generate each of the plurality of hatching strokes according to the randomly- generated stroke lengths; determine a stroke center for each of the plurality of hatching strokes; determine a randomly-generated offset between each hatching stroke in each of the parallel columns; and draw each of the plurality of hatching strokes on the parallel columns based on the randomly-generated offsets. 10. The system of claim 8, wherein the hatching tool is further operative to perturb each of the plurality of hatching strokes along at least one axis according to a randomly- generated deviation. 11. The system of claim 10, wherein in perturbing each of the plurality of hatching strokes along the at least one axis according to the randomly-generated deviation, the hatching tool is operative to: determine the randomly-generated deviation, wherein the randomly-generated deviation is less than or equal to an equal or nearly-equal spacing between the parallel columns; and perturb each of the plurality of hatching strokes along an axis perpendicular to or nearly-perpendicular to the parallel columns according to the randomly-generated deviation. 12. The system of claim 8, wherein the hatching tool is further operative to: calculate endpoints for each of the plurality of hatching strokes; and map the plurality of hatching strokes to the object. 13. The system of claim 12, wherein in mapping the plurality of hatching strokes to the object, the hatching tool is operative to perform at least one of: translate the plurality of hatching strokes to the object; uniformly rotate the plurality of hatching strokes by an angle according to a predetermined or user-selected fill angle; keystone-correct the plurality of hatching strokes according to a predetermined or user-selected gradient; and clip one or more of the plurality of hatching strokes to a boundary defining the object. 14. The system of claim 8, wherein the hatching tool is further operative to generate a plurality of overstrokes positioned over a portion of the plurality of hatching strokes, wherein each overstroke is a darker but narrower stroke than the hatching stroke over which the overstroke is positioned. 15. A computer readable storage device including computer readable instructions, which when executed by a computer provides a hatching tool operative to: receive an indication of a selection to apply sketch-effect hatching to an object; determine a bounding area associated with the object for generating hatching strokes; 

 within the bounding area, define a plurality of equally or near-equally spaced parallel columns; determine a randomly-generated stroke length for each of a plurality of hatching strokes; generate each of the plurality of hatching strokes according to the randomly- generated stroke lengths; determine a stroke center for each of the plurality of hatching strokes; determine a randomly-generated offset between each hatching stroke in each of the parallel columns; draw each of the plurality of hatching strokes on the parallel columns based on the randomly-generated offsets; perturb each of the plurality of hatching strokes according to a randomly-generated angle deviation; perturb each of the plurality of hatching strokes along at least one axis according to a randomly-generated deviation; calculate endpoints for each of the plurality of hatching strokes; generate a plurality of overstrokes positioned over a portion of the plurality of hatching strokes, wherein each overstroke is a darker but narrower stroke than the hatching stroke over which the overstroke is positioned; map the plurality of hatching strokes and overstrokes to the object; and provide an output of the plurality of hatching strokes and overstrokes to a visual display for rendering the plurality of hatching strokes on the visual display or to a printing device for printing the object with the plurality of hatching strokes and overstrokes to a print medium.
